g.text: THE DEBOUNCE NETWORK - AND WHAT IT DELIBERATELY DOES NOT DO

g.desc: THE DEBOUNCE NETWORK - AND WHAT IT DELIBERATELY DOES NOT DO

g.text: rising edge, switch opens tau = R3 x C3 = 4.7 kohm x 100 nF = 0.47 ms

g.desc: rising edge, switch opens tau = R3 x C3 = 4.7 kohm x 100 nF = 0.47 ms

g.text: falling edge, switch closes tau = (R3 || R4) x C3 = 427 ohm x 100 nF = 43 us

g.desc: falling edge, switch closes tau = (R3 || R4) x C3 = 427 ohm x 100 nF = 43 us

g.text: WHAT R4 IS FOR, and it is not the time constant. Without it, C3 discharges

g.desc: WHAT R4 IS FOR, and it is not the time constant. Without it, C3 discharges

g.text: directly through the switch contacts and the peak current is limited only by

g.desc: directly through the switch contacts and the peak current is limited only by

g.text: contact and wire resistance - an amp or more into a few milliohms, on every

g.desc: contact and wire resistance - an amp or more into a few milliohms, on every

g.text: single press. That is how a cheap switch welds itself shut. R4 caps it:

g.desc: single press. That is how a cheap switch welds itself shut. R4 caps it:

g.text: Ipeak = 3.3 V / 470 ohm = 7.0 mA

g.desc: Ipeak = 3.3 V / 470 ohm = 7.0 mA

g.text: It also fixes the closed-state voltage at a defined 0.30 V rather than at

g.desc: It also fixes the closed-state voltage at a defined 0.30 V rather than at

g.text: whatever the contact resistance happens to be that day.

g.desc: whatever the contact resistance happens to be that day.

g.text: WHAT THIS NETWORK DOES NOT DO: it does not debounce the switch. Mechanical

g.desc: WHAT THIS NETWORK DOES NOT DO: it does not debounce the switch. Mechanical

g.text: contacts chatter for 1-10 ms. A 0.47 ms / 43 us pair of time constants filters

g.desc: contacts chatter for 1-10 ms. A 0.47 ms / 43 us pair of time constants filters

g.text: sub-millisecond chatter and guarantees a monotonic edge into the pin, and that

g.desc: sub-millisecond chatter and guarantees a monotonic edge into the pin, and that

g.text: is exactly what it is sized to do - no more. Making the RC big enough to swallow

g.desc: is exactly what it is sized to do - no more. Making the RC big enough to swallow

g.desc: 10 ms of bounce on its own would need roughly 100 uF here, and would blur a real

g.desc: press into something firmware could no longer time.

g.desc: The multi-millisecond settle is firmware's job, and the choice between polling

g.desc: worth stating exactly: the pin sees a clean monotonic transition with no

g.desc: sub-microsecond glitches, and it never floats.

g.desc: it - because that is what a light switch is. It has no released position to

g.text: The consequence for firmware: the event is an EDGE, in either direction, and

g.desc: The consequence for firmware: the event is an EDGE, in either direction, and

g.text: both directions mean the same thing, "toggle". There is no press-and-release

g.desc: both directions mean the same thing, "toggle". There is no press-and-release

g.text: pair to match up, and "up is on" stops meaning anything the moment something
